name: Pig Roast--Pit Style
recipeIngredient: 1 whole suckling pig, approximately 10 pounds or more, gutted, cleaned, scraped, and with feet removed
1 very large pork roast, weighing at least 10 pounds, rinsed
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the barbecue pit by digging a hole 2-3 feet deep and 2 feet wide. Use one 15-pound bag of good-brand charcoal for the bottom layer. If the ground is wet, line with stones first.
2. After thoroughly rinsing the pig, place it in a soup pot or large crock and pour over the vinegar. Soak for 30 minutes to an hour, turning twice.
3. Drain the pig and reserve 1 cup of the vinegar. Rub the pig thoroughly inside and out with plenty of salt, pepper, and granulated garlic. You can also make small knife-slits in the surface of the pig and insert slivers of fresh garlic.
4. Wrap the pig for roasting by stacking three full layers of heavy-duty aluminum foil, tearing off pieces about 8 inches longer than the pig on either side. Lightly grease the inside of the foil with margarine or oil. Fold the foil around the pig to make a package, rolling the layers together at the seams to create a very tight seal all around. Wrap again tightly in another layer of foil.
5. Start the charcoal in the pit. Once it begins to ash over, cover with a thin layer of medium-sized stones or bricks. Place the wrapped pig on top and surround with the rest of the charcoal. Allow it to cook until the second layer of charcoal ashes over.
6. Turn the pig using two spatulas and remove it from the pit onto a platter. Partially unwrap it and test for doneness by cutting into the thigh next to the bone. It should be very well done (white) and pull off easily. Check the rib cavity to ensure the meat is uniformly white (well done) and shreds easily, not pinkish. If underdone, rewrap the pig and place in a hot oven (400°F) for one hour or until thoroughly cooked.
7. Place the pig on a platter and garnish with parsley and either radishes or candied crab apples. Carve and serve.
8. Alternate excellent accompaniments include fresh pineapple slices, fruit salad, mashed butternut squash with butter and nutmeg, vinegar-dressed cole slaw, small whole garlic-roasted or boiled potatoes, "King's Hawaiian" bread or rolls.
Note: If you don't live in the country and can't dig a hole in your yard, prepare this dish in a smoker. Ensure it cooks for at least 10 hours and replenish charcoal supply every 3-4 hours to maintain ideal heat. Start early in the day.
